data.title: Treasury Yields Have Another Rising Week Amid U.S.-Iran Standoff — Market Talk
data.description: Treasury yields rise in a week marked by robust U.S. indicators and dimming hopes of an end to tanker traffic obstruction in the Strait of Hormuz. Markets will focus on labor data next week. Fed officials express concerns over war-induced inflation. Citi analysts expect little energy inflation pass-through, leaving room for the Fed to react to a potential rise in unemployment. Citi expects the Fed to cut rates by 75 basis points this year. The 10-year yield rises 0.069 percentage point this week, to 4.377% and the two-year rises 0.112 point to 3.887%. Both are little changed on the day. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Correction to How These New Funds Squeeze Article — WSJ
data.description: Matt Kaufman, head of ETFs at Calamos, says: "You're going to get paid 14% on a monthly basis as long as [the reference index tied to the S&P 500] doesn't go down 40% or more." "How These New Funds Squeeze 14% Yields Out of Stocks," at 10 a.m. ET, incorrectly quoted Kaufman saying that the fund's yield depends on the S&P 500.

data.title: T Rowe Price Is Maintained at Hold by TD Cowen
data.description: (19:39 GMT) T Rowe Price Price Target Raised to $94.00/Share From $93.00 by TD Cowen

data.title: Airlines Prepping for Potential Spirit Shutdown — WSJ
data.description:
By Jacob Passy

Competitors of Spirit Airlines are readying their game plans in the event the budget carrier ceases operations.

American Airlines has already begun putting caps on fares for economy-class tickets on nonstop routes that overlap with Spirit's network, a spokeswoman said. "The American Airlines team is prepared to do all it can to support Spirit Airlines customers andteam members," she said.

A United Airlines spokesperson said the company was preparing to support Spirit employees and customers, adding that it would provide additional details at a later time.

Frontier Airlines, which overlaps with Spirit on more than 100 routes, plans to "focus on helping people continue their travel plans with low-fare options," a spokeswoman said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Axia Energia SA expected to post earnings of R$1.25 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Axia Energia SA , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Rio De Janeiro-based company is expected to report a 17.3% increase in revenue to R$12.216 billion from R$10.41 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Axia Energia SA is for earnings of R$1.25 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

data.title: Axia Energia SA expected to post earnings of 29 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Axia Energia SA , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on May 5 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Rio De Janeiro-based company is expected to report a 27.8% increase in revenue to $2.275 billion from $1.78 billion a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Axia Energia SA is for earnings of 29 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Axia Energia SA is $13.50, about 7.6% above its last closing price of $12.55

data.title: Airlines Seen Ready to Fill Void Left by Spirit — Market Talk
data.description: If Spirit Airlines does liquidate its business, as The Wall Street Journal reported it is planning to do, other airlines are likely going to jump in to fill the gap it creates, Melius analyst write in a note. "When Spirit filed its second bankruptcy in September 2025, Frontier, JetBlue, and United all made concerted efforts to backfill Spirit's markets out of Florida," the analysts write. "We expect all three to be even more aggressive now given the potential jump ball for assets and routes." But though Frontier and JetBlue saw the biggest stock price gains following the Journal's report, the analysts see Delta and United as benefiting the most from structural changes in the industry, with other airlines needing market conditions to ease before catching up. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Treasuries Move Modestly Higher, Extending Yesterday's Rebound
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Treasuries saw modest strength during trading on Friday, extending the rebound seen over the course of the previous session.

Bond prices gave back ground after an early advance but remained in positive territory. As a result, the yield on the benchmark ten-year note, which moves opposite of its price, dipped 1.2 basis points to 4.378 percent.

The ten-year yield added to the 2.8 basis point drop seen on Thursday, pulling back further off Wednesday's one-month closing high.

Treasuries showed a strong move to the upside following the release of a report from the Institute for Supply Management U.S. manufacturing activity continued to expand in April but at a slightly slower than expected pace.

The ISM said its manufacturing PMI came in at 52.7 in April, unchanged from March. While a reading above 50 still indicates growth, economists had expected the index to inch up to 53.0.

The strength among treasuries also came amid an extended pullback by the price of crude oil, with U.S. crude oil futures tumbling by nearly 3 percent.

U.S. crude oil futures showed a significant downturn on Thursday, slumping by 1.7 percent after reaching their highest levels in four years.

The continued decrease by crude oil prices came amid reports Iran has delivered its response to the latest U.S. amendments on the agreement to end the war through Pakistani mediators.

Traders seemed to shrug off the response from President Donald Trump, who told reporters at the White House that he was 'not satisfied' with Iran's proposal.

data.title: Change in Fed Leadership May Hamper Crypto Rebound — Market Talk
data.description: Analysts are speculating over how changes in policy style and substance under incoming Fed chair Kevin Warsh will impact markets. For cryptocurrency, a looser schedule for updating the market may hamper investor's money flowand keep a lid on cryptocurrency movement. "In a world where employment is still firm and energy prices are keeping inflation high, the short-term setup for crypto looks tougher," says Galaxy Research in a note. "Policy uncertainty is back, the near-term outlook is hawkish, and markets may have to adjust to trading without the Fed's macro narrative every six weeks." Bitcoin is up 2.7% to $78,492, ethereum rises 1.9% to $2,306, XRP is up 1.9% to $1.40, and solana rises 1.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)
